definition: Generate candidates for expensive structured edit distances using lower bounds, q-gram or subtree indexes, metric indexes, sorted upper bounds, embeddings, or structural signatures, then verify or rerank with the desired graph, tree, JSON, AST, or phylogenetic edit distance.

scale_profile.best_for: Structured scientific units where exact edit distance is meaningful but too expensive for all train-eval pairs.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Thousands to millions of structured objects depending on graph size, label vocabulary, and bound tightness.
scale_profile.avoid_when: The structure can be faithfully represented as a simple vector or when exact edit distance is not scientifically justified.

tunable_parameters.name: edit_cost_model
tunable_parameters.purpose: Defines insertion, deletion, substitution, and attribute costs.

tunable_parameters.name: candidate_threshold_or_k
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls the candidate set size before exact verification.

tunable_parameters.name: structural_signature
tunable_parameters.purpose: Selects q-grams, subtree features, anchors, embeddings, or metric pivots.

quality_gates: Report the edit cost model because it is part of the scientific similarity definition.
Verify that lower bounds are admissible when exact threshold guarantees are claimed.
Compare candidate recall against brute-force edit distance on a small subset.
failure_modes: Generic structural signatures can miss domain-critical attributes.
Non-uniform edit costs may break assumptions made by an index or lower bound.
Approximate candidates can systematically miss unusual structures in the novel SPECTRA bins.
example_usage: Use string_qgram_edit_join.py only as a minimal filter-and-verify template; structured implementations should replace q-grams and Levenshtein verification with graph, tree, JSON, AST, or phylogeny-specific distance code.

citations.title: Metric Indexing for Graph Similarity Search
citations.evidence: Uses metric indexing and lower/upper bounds for graph similarity search under graph edit distance variants.

citations.title: Efficient Graph Edit Distance Computation and Verification via Anchor-aware Lower Bound Estimation
citations.evidence: Introduces anchor-aware lower bounds to reduce graph edit-distance verification cost.

citations.title: JEDI: These aren't the JSON documents you're looking for
citations.evidence: Defines an edit-based JSON distance and uses indexing and bounds to accelerate similarity queries over structured documents.
